task_completion.description: Update the admin user's roles to include the can_delete capability.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk web interface showing the 'Roles' or 'Users' configuration page where the 'can_delete' capability is being assigned to the admin user or a role held by the admin.

task_completion.description: Search for the specific leaked password events in the web_logs index.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk web interface search bar showing a query targeting the leaked passwords, such as 'index=web_logs sourcetype=apache_leak password='.

task_completion.description: Purge the identified password events using the delete command.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk web interface showing the search query piped to the delete command (e.g., '| delete') and a success message or statistics indicating that the events were successfully deleted.

task_completion.description: Open or create the props.conf file to implement the index-time masking rule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a text editor (like nano or vim) or a cat/echo command modifying '/opt/splunk/etc/system/local/props.conf' with an '[apache_leak]' stanza.

task_completion.description: Add the specific SEDCMD-redact directive to mask future passwords.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the exact string 'SEDCMD-redact = s/password=[^&" ]+/password=REDACTED/g' placed under the '[apache_leak]' stanza in the props.conf file.

task_completion.description: Ensure configuration and permission changes are fully active by restarting the Splunk service.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command to restart Splunk (e.g., '/opt/splunk/bin/splunk restart') or a Splunk web interface message indicating the server is restarting/has restarted.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Splunk web interface for the deletion task as requested.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual Splunk GUI (http://localhost:8000) being used for the search and delete operations, rather than just terminal API/curl commands.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not delete the entire index, but only the password events.
integrity.visual_evidence: The delete command in the Splunk search bar must explicitly include a filter for the password data (e.g., 'password=*' or 'password=') before the '| delete' pipe.

integrity.description: Verify the agent genuinely interacted with the terminal to edit the configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal screenshots should show standard Linux commands and editor interfaces (e.g., nano, vim, cat) modifying the actual file path '/opt/splunk/etc/system/local/props.conf'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target index is 'web_logs' and the sourcetype is 'apache_leak'. The Splunk credentials are admin / SplunkAdmin1!. The redaction rule must exactly match: SEDCMD-redact = s/password=[^&" ]+/password=REDACTED/g in /opt/splunk/etc/system/local/props.conf.
